close_reason: Committed: shared ESLint config (packages/eslint-config/base.js), per-app configs with framework groups (svelte/react), import restrictions banning direct @vex-chat/types + crypto imports. Desktop: svelte parsing works, 0 errors. Mobile: react-hooks added (react plugin deferred — incompatible with ESLint 10), 0 non-hooks errors. Store: 0 errors. verifiedKeys.ts removed (localStorage broken on all platforms).
description: Create packages/eslint-config with base.ts exporting shared config (strictTypeChecked, perfectionist with import grouping, prettier). Each app/package gets its own eslint.config.ts importing the base. Desktop adds svelte plugin + svelte framework group. Mobile adds react + react-hooks + react framework group. Store/UI use base only. ALSO: ban direct imports from @vex-chat/types and @vex-chat/crypto in app code — apps should only import from @vex-chat/libvex or @vex-chat/store. Use no-restricted-imports to enforce.
title: vex-chat: shared ESLint config package + per-app configs

description: Epic covering all phases from IMPLEMENTATION_PLAN.md: compliance gates, Zod migration, codec factory, changesets, protocol specs, contract testing, and docs. All work on protocol-specs branch across types-js, crypto-js, libvex-js, spire, and vex-chat.
title: Protocol Specs v3 — Mega Branch

close_reason: Committed on protocol-specs branch: security hardening, TS strictness, license enforcement, enum→as-const, tweetnacl removed, any→unknown
description: Security hardening (SECURITY.md, CODEOWNERS, dependabot.yml, CodeQL workflow, scorecard workflow, branch protection, pin GHA to SHA, harden-runner), license compliance (license-checker-rspack-2, license:check in CI including devDeps), TS maximum strictness (tsconfig all strict flags, ESLint strictTypeChecked, type-coverage 99%+), package manager enforcement (only-allow npm, engines field), dependency pinning (npm-package-json-lint, save-exact CI env var), README badges, remove tweetnacl devDep/peerDep.

close_reason: Validated: types RC6 published to verdaccio. crypto-js compiles clean. libvex-js compiles (except expected missing crypto types for beads-at4.12). spire compiles (except msg.data unknown — expected from any→unknown). E2E tests: 28 passed, 2 skipped. Gotcha: z.instanceof(Uint8Array) causes ArrayBuffer/ArrayBufferLike generic mismatch in TS 6 — fixed with z.custom<Uint8Array<any>>.

Move ISessionSQL, IIdentityKeys to src/types/storage.ts. Move StoredCredentials, KeyStore to src/types/identity.ts. Update all imports throughout Client.ts, IStorage.ts, storage/*.ts, utils/*.ts.

close_reason: Committed: all types migrated to Zod schemas, split into 7 domain files, ISucessMsg→ISuccessMsg rename, 100% type coverage, isolatedDeclarations removed (incompatible with Zod)
description: Install zod 4.1+. Split src/index.ts into domain schema files (common.ts, users.ts, servers.ts, files.ts, messages.ts, keys.ts). Migrate all interfaces to z.object() with z.infer<typeof> type exports. Use as-const objects for enums (TokenScopes, SocketAuthErrors, MailType). Add hexBytes() codec for wire binary fields. Fix ISucessMsg→ISuccessMsg typo. Add TSDoc + .describe() to all schemas. Keep same exported type names for backward compat.
title: types-js: migrate interfaces to Zod schemas

close_reason: Committed: AsyncAPI 3.0 spec generated from Zod schemas via z.toJSONSchema(). 11 message types (5 send, 6 receive). Exported in npm package as asyncapi.json. Uses built-in Zod v4 JSON Schema conversion — no extra library needed.
description: Create scripts/generate-asyncapi.ts using zod-to-json-schema. Map all WebSocket message types (client→server: auth, challenge, response, resource, receipt, ping; server→client: challenge, authorized, authErr, success, error, notify, ping, pong) to AsyncAPI 3.0 document. Output asyncapi.json committed to repo. Add Spectral linting + @asyncapi/parser validation in CI.
title: types-js: generate AsyncAPI spec from Zod schemas

close_reason: Closed
description: Remove cookie simulation from platform-transports.ts: delete getAxiosCookies(), remove cookie forwarding from NodeTestWS and BrowserTestWS constructors, remove cookie-jar comments. All three transports become identical (bare WebSocket, no auth headers — post-connection auth handles everything).
title: [ADR-008] Clean up test transports

description: Two-phase feature to make React Native work end-to-end. Phase 1: ADR-006 post-connection WS auth (unblocks RN connect). Phase 2: ADR-007 device-key challenge-response auto-login (passwordless resumed sessions with 1-hour JWT). Also separates JWT_SECRET from SPK.
title: feat/rn-auth: Post-connection WS auth + device-key auto-login

close_reason: Closed
description: Validate that after Phase 1, the RN platform test (test:rn) passes register, login, connect, and send/receive DM. Remove the TODO comment from platform-rn.test.ts. This is the gate for Phase 1 completion.
title: [test] RN platform test passes all 4 tests

close_reason: Closed
description: In initSocket(), on 'open' event send JSON.stringify({ type: 'auth', token: this.token }) as first message. Remove the headers: { Cookie } option from WebSocket constructor. Wait for server to accept auth before proceeding. Same code path for all platforms (Node, browser, Tauri, RN).
title: [libvex] Send auth message on WS open

close_reason: Closed
description: Add JWT_SECRET env var for JWT signing. Stop reusing the NaCl server signing key (SPK) for HMAC-SHA256 JWT signatures. Generate with: node -e "console.log(require('crypto').randomBytes(64).toString('hex'))". Fall back to SPK if JWT_SECRET not set (backward compat). Auth-comparison doc flagged this as a key hygiene concern.
title: [spire] Separate JWT_SECRET from SPK

close_reason: Closed
description: Each Client creates its own axios.create() instance instead of using the global ax import. After login/loginWithDeviceKey, set Authorization: Bearer on the per-client instance. Delete addCookie(), getCookies(), this.cookies[], and the isNode check. Remove browser-or-node remnants.
title: [ADR-008] libvex: per-client axios + Bearer headers

close_reason: Closed
description: Add setTimeout(0) yield between initSocket() and negotiateOTK() in connect(). OTK generation creates 100 Ed25519 key pairs synchronously (~5s on mobile), blocking the event loop and preventing the WS open callback from firing. Without the yield, the auth message never sends before the server timeout.
title: [libvex] Event loop yield before OTK generation

close_reason: Closed
description: On wss.on('connection'), wait for first message: { type: 'auth', token: JWT }. 10-second timeout — no auth = close. Valid JWT → create ClientManager, proceed with challenge flow. Invalid → send { type: 'unauthorized' }, close. Replaces the current cookie-based express-ws middleware auth.
title: [spire] Post-connection WS auth handler

close_reason: Closed
description: Device-key challenge-response issues a 1-hour JWT instead of 7-day. The device key can re-auth cheaply, so long-lived tokens are unnecessary exposure. Password login keeps 7-day expiry since re-entering password is expensive for the user.
title: [spire] 1-hour JWT for device-key auth sessions

close_reason: Closed
description: Remove express-ws dependency. Create WebSocketServer({ noServer: true }). Handle HTTP upgrade via server.on('upgrade') → wss.handleUpgrade(). Change initApp() return type from expressWs.Application to express.Application. Remove express-ws and @types/express-ws from deps.
title: [spire] Replace express-ws with raw ws + noServer

close_reason: Closed
description: Two new routes: POST /auth/device accepts { deviceID, signKey }, looks up device, returns { challenge: hex(nonce) }. POST /auth/device/verify accepts { deviceID, challenge, signed }, verifies Ed25519 signature against stored signKey, issues 1-hour JWT + cookie. Challenge stored in-memory with TTL, single-use, same pattern as action tokens.
title: [spire] Device-key challenge endpoints

close_reason: Closed
description: Replace checkAuth middleware with Bearer-only (Authorization header). Remove cookie-parser dependency. Remove all res.cookie() calls from login, register, goodbye, and device-key auth endpoints. Login response returns { user, token } in body only — no Set-Cookie header.
title: [ADR-008] Spire: Bearer-only auth, remove cookies

close_reason: Closed
description: New method that performs the two-step HTTP handshake: POST /auth/device → sign challenge with device private key → POST /auth/device/verify → receive JWT. Sets this.token and cookie. Export from main entrypoint. Used by auto-login when stored credentials have a deviceKey.
title: [libvex] Client.loginWithDeviceKey() method

close_reason: Closed
description: In bootstrap.ts autoLogin(), call client.loginWithDeviceKey(creds.deviceID) before connect(). Falls back to login screen if device key is revoked or challenge fails. No password needed, no JWT persisted — device key re-authenticates each app launch.
title: [store] autoLogin uses loginWithDeviceKey
